title: Port highest-priority superpowers concepts into agent-bober
description:
ABANDONED 2026-05-24 — superseded by spec-20260524-bober-vision which expands scope to verbatim voice port + multi-mode careful-flow + generic prod-incident agent shape. The eight sprints here become a subset of Tiers 0-1 in the vision spec; original contracts remain on disk for reference but are not scheduled for execution.

ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION: Adopt the behavior-shaping disciplines from obra/superpowers into agent-bober's harness: SessionStart bootstrap, Iron Law / Red Flags / Rationalization-Prevention prose patterns, verification-before-completion, systematic-debugging, anti-pattern reference docs, brainstorming HARD-GATE, AGENTS.md contributor contract, and a separate code-review subagent. The harness gains per-agent behavioral discipline without changing its orchestrator data model or contract schema.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Scope is the prose/skill/agent-prompt tier from the research doc (items #1-5, #9, #10, #12, #13, #15 in the Opportunities table). Defer to future specs: worktrees per sprint (#6, orchestrator code), parallel sprint dispatch (#7, orchestrator code), finishing-a-development-branch (#8, lower value for harness), writing-skills adversarial-pressure test methodology (#11, needs its own dedicated spec).

Voice: adopt Iron Law / Red Flags / Rationalization-Prevention table STRUCTURE across agent prompts, but preserve agent-bober's existing procedural voice. Do NOT import 'your human partner' terminology, 'EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT' all-caps blocks, or the 94%-rejection framing. The structural patterns carry the empirical tuning; the surface phrasing is bober's.

SessionStart hook coexistence: extend hooks/hooks.json to register a SessionStart matcher alongside the existing PostToolUse matchers. Preserve whatever currently emits the 'SessionStart:startup hook success: Nodes: 0 ...' graph-stats message — that becomes one block, the bootstrap injection becomes another. No replacement.

Two-stage review: add a new bober-code-reviewer subagent that runs AFTER evaluator pass, rather than splitting the existing evaluator. Smaller orchestrator change, preserves the eval-strategy contract, and keeps code-quality judgment in a fresh-context agent. Failure of the code reviewer does not block sprint completion in this spec — its output is advisory, written to .bober/reviews/<contractId>.md.

Anti-pattern docs sourced from superpowers (MIT) are ported with inline attribution header on every file. Verbatim port is acceptable for the four seed docs (testing-anti-patterns.md, condition-based-waiting.md, root-cause-tracing.md, defense-in-depth.md) since MIT permits this; bober-specific additions go in separate files.

assumptions: Source research is .bober/research/research-20260524-superpowers-vs-agent-bober.md — its Opportunities table is the authoritative candidate list
obra/superpowers is MIT-licensed (per /tmp/superpowers/LICENSE seen during research); verbatim prose ports are permitted with attribution
hooks/hooks.json supports multiple matchers and multiple hooks per matcher; adding SessionStart alongside existing PostToolUse will not collide
Claude Code SessionStart hook ENV provides CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or equivalent so the hook script can find its repo-root anchor — fall back to git-rev-parse if not set
Subagent prompts in agents/*.md are injected verbatim into a spawned subagent's context by the orchestrator; modifying them changes runtime behavior without code changes
The orchestrator can spawn N subagent types (already does six); adding a seventh (bober-code-reviewer) is a localized change
Existing eval-strategy contract (typecheck/lint/build/test) remains the source of truth for sprint pass/fail; code review is advisory in this spec
Anti-pattern docs live at .bober/anti-patterns/ — colocated with other .bober/ runtime artifacts so they ship with the project, not just the harness package
AGENTS.md at repo root is the conventional location agents are auto-instructed to read at session start; placing it here gives the slop-prevention contract maximum reach
outOfScope: Using git-worktrees per sprint — defer to follow-on spec spec-XXX-worktree-isolation
Parallel sprint dispatch for file-disjoint sprints — defer to follow-on spec spec-XXX-parallel-sprints
Splitting bober-evaluator into spec-compliance and code-quality halves — chose additive code-reviewer agent instead
Adversarial-pressure test methodology for skills (the writing-skills meta) — defer to follow-on spec spec-XXX-skill-pressure-tests
finishing-a-development-branch standardized menu — lower value for the harness pipeline
Replacing two-phase researcher with brainstorming — would lose rigor; brainstorming HARD-GATE sits inside bober.plan instead
Plugin marketplace distribution machinery from superpowers (scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh, bump-version.sh) — distribution model mismatch
Migrating existing .bober/ artifacts or contract schema — pure additions only
Importing 'your human partner' terminology — voice choice, not structural pattern

features.title: SessionStart bootstrap injection
features.description: A SessionStart hook script that injects a bober.using-bober skill bootstrap into the model's first-turn context, establishing the skill-invocation discipline before any user message arrives. Coexists with the existing graph-stats SessionStart payload.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When a Claude Code session starts in an agent-bober project, the system reminder contains both the existing graph stats line AND a new bober-using-bober bootstrap block.
AC2: The hook script hooks/session-start exits 0 in under 500ms on a cold cache.
AC3: hooks/hooks.json registers a SessionStart matcher alongside the existing PostToolUse matchers; both fire independently.

AC4: skills/bober.using-bober/SKILL.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter (name, description fields, <=1024 chars total) and a body that names the bober.* skill set, the Iron Law convention, and points to AGENTS.md.

features.title: Verification-before-completion discipline
features.description: An Iron Law gate forbidding completion claims without fresh verification command output, applied to the generator's self-verify phase via a new bober.verify skill and inlined into agents/bober-generator.md.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When the generator finishes implementing a sprint, its prompt now requires running every eval strategy command and including the output in its completion report.
AC2: skills/bober.verify/SKILL.md exists with an Iron Law block, Red Flags table, and Rationalization-Prevention table mapping at least 8 excuses to refutations.
AC3: agents/bober-generator.md contains a fenced Iron Law block ('NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION COMMAND OUTPUT') in its self-verify section and cross-references skills/bober.verify.
AC4: The completion-report JSON schema (referenced from bober-generator.md) now includes a verificationOutput field listing each command run and its exit code.

features.title: Systematic-debugging discipline for evaluator-feedback retries
features.description: A 4-phase root-cause-first investigation discipline applied to the generator's evaluator-feedback path. Forbids fixes before root cause is identified.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: skills/bober.debug/SKILL.md exists with Phase 1 (Root Cause Investigation), Phase 2 (Pattern Analysis), Phase 3 (Fix Hypothesis), Phase 4 (Fix Verification) plus Iron Law and Red Flags.

AC2: agents/bober-generator.md gains a 'when evaluator returns failures' section that requires invoking bober.debug before any code change, and applies the 'receiving code review' discipline (verify before implementing, push back if reviewer is wrong with reasoning).
AC3: Generator prompt explicitly forbids responses like 'You're absolutely right!' to evaluator feedback and requires technical restatement of the requirement before any fix attempt.

features.title: Anti-pattern reference catalog
features.description: Four MIT-attributed anti-pattern docs ported from superpowers, indexed in a README, loaded by the evaluator and referenced by name when failing success criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: .bober/anti-patterns/README.md indexes four docs: testing-anti-patterns.md, condition-based-waiting.md, root-cause-tracing.md, defense-in-depth.md.
AC2: Every anti-pattern file opens with an attribution block citing obra/superpowers and its MIT license.
AC3: agents/bober-evaluator.md instructs the evaluator to reference anti-pattern names from this catalog in the 'regressions' field of its EvalResult when applicable.
AC4: The four anti-pattern files contain the substantive content from their superpowers originals (not stub files).

features.title: Iron Law / Red Flags style pass across remaining agent prompts
features.description: Apply the Iron Law / Red Flags / Rationalization-Prevention table structure consistently across bober-planner, bober-curator, bober-architect, bober-researcher, and bober-evaluator. Generator was already covered by feat-2/feat-3.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Each of the five named agent prompt files contains exactly one fenced Iron Law block stating the agent's single non-negotiable rule (e.g. researcher: 'NO RESEARCH WITHOUT FILE-PATH-AND-LINE-NUMBER EVIDENCE').

AC2: Each of the five agent prompt files contains a Red Flags table listing role-appropriate STOP conditions (planner: 'asking what is obvious from the codebase'; evaluator: 'trusting generator success claim without independent verification'; etc.).
AC3: Each of the five agent prompt files contains a Rationalization-Prevention table with at least 5 rows mapping excuses to refutations.
AC4: Voice consistency check: no agent file uses 'your human partner', 'EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT' all-caps blocks, or the 94%-rejection framing.

features.title: Brainstorming HARD-GATE in bober.plan
features.description: An explicit <HARD-GATE> block in skills/bober.plan/SKILL.md that blocks sprint-contract emission until design approval (interactive: user approval; autonomous: planner self-approves with cited file:line evidence).
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: skills/bober.plan/SKILL.md contains a single <HARD-GATE> tagged block placed between Step 5 (PlanSpec generation) and Step 6 (Sprint decomposition).

AC2: The gate defines two protocols: interactive (user approves/edits the assumptions and outOfScope sections) and autonomous (planner self-approves only if assumptions are each backed by cited evidence with at least file path).
AC3: The gate explicitly forbids writing .bober/contracts/*.json files before approval is recorded in resolvedClarifications or assumptions are evidenced.

features.title: AGENTS.md contributor contract
features.description: An AGENTS.md at repo root that establishes the anti-slop discipline for agent-authored changes to agent-bober itself — what to do before opening a PR, what will not be accepted, evidence requirements. Adapted from superpowers's voice to agent-bober's voice.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: AGENTS.md exists at repo root with sections: 'If You Are an AI Agent', 'Pull Request Requirements', 'What We Will Not Accept', 'Evidence Requirements', 'Skill Changes Require Verification'.
AC2: README.md gains a single one-line link to AGENTS.md in a 'Contributing' section.
AC3: skills/bober.using-bober/SKILL.md cross-references AGENTS.md so it surfaces at session start.
AC4: AGENTS.md preserves the structural pattern (numbered pre-PR checks, do-not-accept list) but uses agent-bober's voice, not superpowers's '94% rejection rate' framing.

features.title: bober-code-reviewer agent + bober.code-review skill + orchestrator wiring
features.description: A new fresh-context subagent that runs after the evaluator passes a sprint, performing code-quality review (separate from rubric-based eval). Writes an advisory report; does not block sprint completion in this spec. Requires a localized orchestrator change to spawn the new agent.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: agents/bober-code-reviewer.md exists with tools restricted to Read/Bash/Grep/Glob (no Write/Edit), following the bober-evaluator pattern.
AC2: skills/bober.code-review/SKILL.md exists with a Process Flow section describing what the reviewer checks (DRY, YAGNI, surprising patterns, missing tests, dead code, anti-pattern references).

AC3: After bober-evaluator returns overallResult='pass' for a sprint, the orchestrator spawns bober-code-reviewer with the same contract and the eval result; the reviewer produces .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md.
AC4: The review file follows a documented schema (markdown with sections: Summary, Critical, Important, Minor, Approved). At minimum the Summary section must be populated.
AC5: If code review reports Critical issues, the orchestrator surfaces them in the run-summary but does NOT block sprint completion in this spec — output is advisory.
AC6: The new orchestrator code path has unit test coverage (at least one test that mocks evaluator pass and asserts code-reviewer is spawned).
AC7: Existing eval strategies (typecheck, lint, build, unit-test) all still pass on the agent-bober repo itself after the change.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All changes must be additive — no existing .bober/ artifact, contract, or eval result is mutated retroactively; no existing skill, agent prompt, or hook is removed.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Run `git diff main -- .bober/ skills/ agents/ hooks/` after each sprint; verify no DELETED skill/agent/hook entries (modifications and additions are fine).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Every prose port from superpowers retains an inline MIT attribution block citing obra/superpowers and the source file path.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Grep each new file in .bober/anti-patterns/ and skills/bober.{verify,debug,code-review,using-bober}/ for the string 'obra/superpowers' and confirm presence.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: SessionStart hook completes in under 500ms on cold cache to avoid noticeable session-start latency.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: time bash hooks/session-start (run twice; second run is cold-cache representative); assert <500ms wall-clock.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Agent prompt files remain readable end-to-end at under 600 lines each after the Iron Law style pass.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: wc -l agents/*.md; assert each file <=600 lines.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All existing evaluator strategy commands (typecheck, lint, build, test) continue to pass on the agent-bober repo itself after every sprint.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Run npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test after each sprint; assert exit 0.

integrationPoints: agents/*.md — five prompts modified, one new (bober-code-reviewer)
skills/ — four new skills (bober.using-bober, bober.verify, bober.debug, bober.code-review)
.bober/anti-patterns/ — new directory with four ported docs
src/orchestrator/ — one localized change: spawn code-reviewer after evaluator pass
AGENTS.md — new file at repo root
techNotes.dataModel: No new persistent data model. Two new artifact paths: .bober/anti-patterns/*.md (static reference) and .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md (per-sprint advisory output from code-reviewer).
securityConsiderations: SessionStart hook reads SKILL.md from disk and emits it via stdout JSON; ensure JSON escaping is correct (model superpowers's escape_for_json bash function)
MIT attribution required on every ported file to comply with upstream license
techNotes.existingPatterns: Follow the structure in agents/bober-evaluator.md (read-only tools list, subagent context block, structured JSON response contract). Hook script follows /tmp/superpowers/hooks/session-start as reference. Skill files follow YAML-frontmatter + markdown pattern already established in skills/bober.*.

metadata.riskNotes: Mostly additive prose changes. Highest-risk sprint is the last (orchestrator wiring for code-reviewer) — and even that is a localized addition of a new subagent spawn after an existing decision point, not a refactor of pipeline flow.
